titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the John Mitzel papers
titlestmt.author: by Emma Gronbeck

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday May 28, 2022 at 03:10

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains material accumulated by John Mitzel in the course of his life and career as a writer, publisher, bookseller, and activist. Included are correspondence and research files on topics and individuals relevant to the LGBTQ community, gay rights, pornography, obscenity laws, and child sexual abuse trials; drafts and clippings of Mitzel's writings and records of the operations and legal disputes of the Glad Day Bookstore and Calamus Books; diaries and scrapbooks spanning several decades; personal and family papers; photographs; and audiovisual and electronic media. Correspondents include Boyd McDonald, Randy Shilts, Charley Shively, David Thorstad, and Gore Vidal. The collection also documents Mitzel's involvement with organizations including the Fag Rag collective and the North American Man/Boy Love Association (NAMBLA), as well as other pro-pedophilia and pederasty organizations.

bioghist.head: John C. Mitzel (1948-2013)
bioghist.p: John Carl Mitzel was an American writer, publisher, bookseller, and activist who was prominently involved in the Boston LGBTQ community beginning in the 1970s. Born in Cincinnati, Ohio, Mitzel attended Boston University and helped to organize Boston's first gay pride parade in 1971. He was a founding member of the Fag Rag collective, which published the newspaper Fag Rag , and was a regular contributor to the Philadelphia Gay News , Gay Community News (Boston), and The Guide (Boston). Mitzel operated the Boston branch of Toronto's Glad Day Bookshop from 1985 until 2000, when he closed the store and opened the bookstore and publisher Calamus Books. He also founded Stonewall Distributors, a gay book and magazine distributor, and Manifest Destiny Press. In addition to his writing and activism for gay rights, Mitzel was also an advocate for the decriminalization of pedophilia and pederasty. He wrote extensively on the subject, served as editor for the North American Man/Boy Love Association (NAMBLA) newsletter, and advocated for and corresponded with men incarcerated for sex crimes. He died on October 4, 2013 in Arlington, Massachusetts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains material accumulated by John Mitzel in the course of his life and career as a writer, publisher, bookseller, and activist. Included are correspondence and research files on topics and individuals relevant to the LGBTQ community, gay rights, pornography, obscenity laws, and child sexual abuse trials; drafts and clippings of Mitzel's writings and records of the operations and legal disputes of the Glad Day Bookstore and Calamus Books; diaries and scrapbooks spanning several decades; personal and family papers; photographs; and audiovisual and electronic media. Correspondents include Boyd McDonald, Randy Shilts, Charley Shively, David Thorstad, and Gore Vidal. The collection also documents Mitzel's involvement with organizations including the Fag Rag collective and the North American Man/Boy Love Association (NAMBLA), as well as other pro-pedophilia and pederasty organizations.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized into eight series: I. Correspondence and Research Files, 1962-2013. II. Subject Files, 1941-2013. III. Professional Papers, 1964-2013. IV. Diaries, 1961-2002. V. Scrapbooks, 1972-2002. VI. Personal Papers, 1959-2012. VII. Photographs, 1924-2012. VIII. Computer and Audiovisual Media, 1971-1999.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit. Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing. This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The John Mitzel Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2013, and gift of the estate of John Mitzel, 2014.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: John Mitzel Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.
Box 51 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 52-54 (audiovisual media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of personal and professional correspondence and research files on individuals and organizations John Mitzel found noteworthy.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Alphabetical Files, and Chronological Files.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains files related to family, friends, and colleagues of John Mitzel, historical figures, LGBTQ rights activists and opponents, and organizations such as Sydney Boy Lovers and Philadelphia Gay News . Materials include original and photocopied correspondence, writings, photographs, and clippings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by surname or organization name, with unsorted and unidentified correspondence filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: Aldyne, Nathan (Michael McDowell and Dennis Shuetz)
Austin, Dorothy and Jim (aunt and uncle)
Groat, David, interview by John Mitzel

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence and related enclosures, such as photographs, writings, printed ephemera, and clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains files on topics of interest to John Mitzel, and groups and issues with which he was personally involved, including LGBTQ rights, the anti-pornography movement, sex crimes and legislation, arrests for child molestation, the North American Man/Boy Love Association (NAMBLA), pro-pedophilia organizations, censorship laws and cases, child pornography cases, and pederasty. Files contain materials such as clippings, newspapers and periodicals, correspondence, writings, financial records, and legal documents.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by file title with unsorted material filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: Boston Lesbian and Gay Political Alliance (BL/GPA)
"Real shits" (Jonestown massacre, Mark Lane, Roy Cohn, conservatives)
"The sex industry (war on the Combat Zone)"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains records related to John Mitzel's career, including writings, publishing and bookstore records, files on other organizations, newsletters, and printed material.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Writings, Bookstores and Publishing, and Printed Material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains drafts and clippings of John Mitzel's writings, including books, reviews, and articles published in a variety of newspapers and periodicals, including the Philadelphia Gay News , Gay Community News , and The Guide . Also present are works published by Mitzel under the pen name Bunny LaRue and a small amount of writings of others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title with untitled works, reviews, and writings of others filed at the end of the sequence.

title.part: Las Adventuras de Juan Carlos en Ohio, Augusto 1997

did.unittitle: "An Approach to Gay Sensibility in Literature," drafts
"An Approach to Gay Sensibility in Literature," research
"The Boy Who Spoke in Pornography," drafts

Boyd McDonald's Letters to John Mitzel, 1975-1989, Part II

"The Girl Who Never Went Anywhere," drafts
"God's Bullies: Power, Politics, and Religious Tyranny," drafts
"The Gum-Chewing Jewess from New York," draft
"Initial proposal for a book by Thomas Reeves and John Mitzel"

John Horne Burns, An Appreciative Biography , book cover
Review of John Horne Burns' The Galley, clippings
"A Time to Remember: John Horne Burns," draft
"June Wedding and Passive Pretty People--The Soft Underbelly of Humanity," draft
"The Lessons of Gay Pride Week: Beware the SWP"

title.part: Letters from Boyd McDonald to Charley Shively, 1976-1989
Letters from Victor Chapin to John Mitzel, 1973-1983

did.unittitle: "Little Turds: A Story of David and Julie Cutenesse"

title.part: Mas Refleciones De Juan Carlo en Ohio, Segundo Meditationes

did.unittitle: "Miami and the Siege of the 14th Amendment"
"The Most Expensive Bl*w J*b in the Commonwealth: The Witch Trial of Dr. Donald Allen," draft
"An Ounce of Prevention Takes Its Pound of Flesh"
"Prescott Townsend: Renegade from Respectability," drafts and notes

title.part: A Short History of Modern Capitalism Through Its Ladies

did.unittitle: "Society's Images, Myths, and Stereotypes Pertaining to Homosexuals," drafts
"Some Short Stories About Nasty People I Don't Like," drafts
"Ten Selections from the 1920s Diaries of Famous Paris Lesbians"
Burns, John Horne, "A Tree of Comfort"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains records of John Mitzel's publishing and bookselling enterprises, such as Glad Day Bookstore and Calamus Books. Files include financial records, bookstore newsletters, notices from the United States Postal Service about confiscated materials, and event planning materials.

did.unittitle: "Customs seizure cases and letters of support"
Diaz, Veronica and Christopher Hall, "Business Logistics Field Based Project"
WAG newsletter, U.K. public lending right/Brigid Brosky

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains published copies of John Mitzel's works; pamphlets and small books produced by Mitzel's publishing imprint, Manifest Destiny Books; magazines and periodicals containing articles by or about Mitzel; and copies of newspapers edited, published, or collected by Mitzel, including Fag Rag and the NAMBLA Newsletter .

did.unittitle: The Age Taboo , ed. Daniel Tsang
Artistry, Community, and Legacy , by Raymond W. Thomas and David P. Mitzel
The Boston Sex Scandal by John Mitzel

title.part: Bunny LaRue's Jackie and Judy Journal

did.unittitle: The Creative Writer in the 20th Century by John Horne Burns
Dead Enz by Kyle G. Brixton [and John Mitzel]
Hooray for Hollywood by Jim Heimann
John Horne Burns: An appreciative autobiography by John Mitzel
Myra and Gore by John Mitzel
On the Line , ed. Ian Young
A Short History of Modern Capitalism Through Its Ladies by John Mitzel
Some Short Stories About Nasty People I Don't Like by John Mitzel
Sports and the Macho Male by John Mitzel

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains several decades of John Mitzel's personal diaries in spiral-bound notebooks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains several decades of John Mitzel's scrapbooks, which include writings, photographs, and newspaper clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains John Mitzel's family papers and memorabilia, high school records and yearbook, personal financial records, and registration and identification records.

did.unittitle: "Notes on the horoscope of John Carl Mitzel"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes photographic prints, negatives, slides, and transparencies. Images depict John Mitzel, Mitzel's friends and family, locations Mitzel visited, various events attended by Mitzel, other gay rights activists and writers, notable public figures, North American Man/Boy Love Association (NAMBLA) meetings and members, Boston gay pride parades, and other subjects.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by file title, with unsorted photographs filed at the end of the sequence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes optical disks, floppy disks, audiocassettes, and videocassettes containing digital copies of writings and recordings of interviews, talks, lectures, meetings, and readings by John Mitzel and others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Computer Media, and Audiovisual Media.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 51 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 52-54 (audiovisual media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains audio recordings of interviews, talks, lectures, meetings, and readings by John Mitzel and other gay writers and activists. Also includes several video recordings of short documentaries and talks about Gore Vidal and Edmund White.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged under two subheadings: Audiocassettes, and VHS Tapes. Each is arranged alphabetically by title.
